unit_name: Limits and an Introduction to Calculus (H)
hook: You already know how to find the slope of a line and the area of simple shapes — but what happens when curves get involved? Algebra alone hits a wall when things are changing continuously, and that's exactly the gap calculus was invented to fill. In this unit, you'll discover the powerful idea that makes it possible to analyze motion, growth, and change with precision.
concept: Every topic in this unit revolves around the concept of approaching — asking what happens to a function as inputs get infinitely close to a value, which leads to the formal idea of a limit and unlocks the two foundational operations of calculus: the derivative and the integral.

topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to describe the behavior of a function as its input approaches a specific value, building the core intuition behind all of calculus.
Master algebraic techniques — including factoring, rationalizing, and the squeeze theorem — to calculate limits precisely without relying solely on graphs or tables.
Apply the concept of one-sided limits to determine whether a function is continuous at a point and identify the types of discontinuities that can occur.

topic_roadmap.topic_name: Infinite Limits and Limits at Infinity (H)
topic_roadmap.description: You will learn to analyze function behavior near vertical asymptotes and as $x \to \pm\infty$, connecting limit notation to the end behavior you studied in earlier units.
You will use the limit definition of the derivative to calculate instantaneous rates of change and find the slope of a curve at any given point.
Explore how limits of sums can be used to find exact areas under curves, giving you your first look at the integral and the remarkable connection between area and accumulation.
